The receiving-center workup bundle ran the bedside repeat twelve-lead-ECG confirming the anterior-wall ST-elevation picture plus the rapid serum-troponin draw plus the basic-metabolic-panel and coagulation-panel and type-and-screen and lipid-panel baseline plus the dual-antiplatelet load with aspirin and a ticagrelor loading-dose plus the heparin anticoagulation bolus plus the chest-X-ray pulmonary-edema screen plus the bedside transthoracic-echocardiogram TTE wall-motion-abnormality survey on the anterior-wall hypokinesis pattern plus the IV access pair-of-bores plus the cardiac-monitor pad placement on the arrhythmia-surveillance cycle. surprise_log.observation: A tsunami warning issued by the National-Tsunami-Warning-Center NTWC in Palmer-Alaska in coordination with the NWS Anchorage Alaska-Region forecast-office and the US-Geological-Survey USGS National-Earthquake-Information-Center NEIC posted onto the regional alert-cadence covering the Aleutian-trench-adjacent Alaskan-coast and the Pacific-West-Coast Cascadia-and-California coastal-margin band on the trailing-minute issuance window following a magnitude-seven-point-eight subduction-zone trench-rupture event at a fifteen-kilometer shallow-focal-depth on the Aleutian-trench seismic-source corridor near the Andreanof-Islands segment: the NEIC moment-tensor solution surfaces a thrust-faulting mechanism on the Pacific-plate-subducting-under-North-American-plate megathrust-interface segment consistent with a tsunami-generating sea-floor displacement profile plus the NTWC tsunami-source-and-wave-height modeling pass surfaces an initial-wave amplitude in the one-to-three-meter range on the near-source Aleutian-Islands shore-impact zone plus a refraction-and-shoaling-amplified runup band on the Alaskan Gulf-of-Alaska coastal-margin in the two-to-four-meter range plus an attenuated Pacific-basin trans-oceanic propagation pattern reaching the Cascadia and Northern-California coast in the trailing-three-to-five-hour wave-arrival window in the half-to-one-meter range. The tsunami-warning-cycle sequencing threads the initial-tsunami-warning-issuance step on the NTWC product-cycle covering the Aleutian-Islands plus Alaskan-Gulf-of-Alaska shore-impact band plus the WEA wireless-emergency-alert mobile-push plus the EAS emergency-broadcast-system loop plus the NOAA-Weather-Radio All-Hazards broadcast plus the per-coastal-community tsunami-siren activation step on the per-municipality tsunami-warning-system audible-alert cadence plus the inundation-zone evacuation-execution step on the per-municipality tsunami-evacuation-route signage and the per-municipality pre-designated tsunami-vertical-evacuation-structure intake plus the per-municipality tsunami-evacuation-assembly-area sheltering step plus the wave-arrival-and-runup observation-and-tide-gauge measurement step on the DART-buoy Deep-ocean-Assessment-and-Reporting-of-Tsunamis fleet and the per-coastal-community tide-gauge fleet on the per-station amplitude-and-arrival-time observation pass plus the post-event search-and-rescue and damage-assessment recovery step on the per-municipality emergency-operations-center EOC mobilization and the regional incident-management-team IMT type-three deployment and the FEMA-Region-Ten federal-disaster-response activation cycle plus the warning-stand-down or extended-warning continuation step on the NTWC update-product-cycle covering the post-wave-arrival observation-based reassessment and the cascading-arrival-and-resurgence wave-train continuation pattern across the trailing-six-to-twelve-hour observation window. The coordinated multi-jurisdictional response cadence threads the Alaska-State Division-of-Homeland-Security-and-Emergency-Management DHSEM activation plus the Washington-State Military-Department Emergency-Management Division activation plus the Oregon-Department-of-Emergency-Management activation plus the California-Office-of-Emergency-Services Cal-OES activation plus the US-Coast-Guard maritime-search-and-rescue and harbor-and-port closure-cycle activation on the per-port harbor-master coordination across the West-Coast port-fleet including Anchorage and Dutch-Harbor and Seattle and Portland and Oakland and Los-Angeles-and-Long-Beach port-and-harbor systems.
